01 — About the Firm

Established in Perak on 23 January 2023, with a clear vision: to become a premier law firm known for legal excellence, integrity, and a client-centred approach.

As a multi-practice boutique firm, our journey began in Perak and gained momentum with our first branch in Langkawi, Kedah, on 1 October 2023. Reflecting our growth, we relocated the Perak branch to Penang Island on 7 April 2025 as part of our expansion plan.

We offer a comprehensive suite of legal services delivered to the highest standards. By understanding each client's needs and priorities, we craft effective solutions aligned with their goals — fostering close, collaborative relationships in every matter.

We are committed to long-term partnerships built on trust, consistently striving to exceed expectations and deliver results that make a meaningful impact.

02 — What We Do

Practice Areas

A wide range of legal services across contentious and non-contentious matters. We continually expand into new areas, and welcome clients to consult us on any matter they face.

i.

Litigation & Dispute Resolution

Civil, commercial, corporate and general litigation; local and international arbitration; mediation; and judicial review.

ii.

Banking & Finance Litigation

Recovery proceedings, execution of judgments, bankruptcy, insolvency, liquidation, foreclosures and auctions.

iii.

Property & Conveyancing

Sale and purchase of properties, loan documentation, leases and tenancies, investment acquisitions and property development.

iv.

Banking & Finance

Retail and corporate banking, securities and capital market instruments, property and project finance, and Islamic banking.

v.

Corporate & Commercial

Contract drafting, mergers and acquisitions, corporate finance, and due diligence.

vi.

Criminal Matters

Advisory and court representation for commercial crimes, offences against persons and property, bailable and non-bailable offences.

vii.

Employment & Labour

Employment contracts, performance and disciplinary matters, Industrial Court representation, and workplace health and safety.

viii.

Communications & Technology

Regulatory compliance, operational contracts, media and entertainment law, IT, and e-commerce.

ix.

Family & Matrimony

Adoption, custody, divorce, distribution of property, and maintenance claims.

x.

Wills, Trusts & Probate

Personal and commercial trusts, grants of probate, letters of administration, and transfer & transmission of property.

A growing practice

We are constantly expanding our expertise into new areas of law — speak to us about your matter.

Panel

Acting for Public Bank, Public Islamic Bank, RHB Bank & RHB Islamic Bank.

03 — The People

Our Lawyers

A team of skilled and dedicated lawyers committed to guiding clients through any legal challenge with expertise and determination.

Partner · Commissioner for Oaths

Quah Chien Chieh

LL.B (Hons) Leeds / Dip. Arbitration / CLP / Advocate & Solicitor, Malaya

Admitted to the Malaysian Bar in November 2013, Mr. Quah brings a diverse background across litigation, banking and corporate law. He began practice in Kuala Lumpur — at Mah Kamariyah & Philip Koh and later Y.Y. Ho & Lee — gaining experience in commercial disputes, intellectual property, construction and general litigation.

From 2014 he specialised in banking and financial law at Ramli Amar Jit & Tan in Alor Setar, handling bankruptcy, foreclosure, winding-up and execution proceedings. He went on to co-found Lim Aik Quah & Co., leading its Langkawi branch, before joining Cindy Ng & Co. as a partner in January 2025. He holds a Diploma in Arbitration and is an appointed Commissioner for Oaths.

LitigationBanking & FinanceArbitration / ADRCorporate
Founder & Partner

Cindy Ng

LL.B (Hons) London / CLP / Advocate & Solicitor, Malaya

The founder of Cindy Ng & Co., Ms. Cindy was admitted to the Malaysian Bar in November 2017. She read in chambers at Srimurugan & Co., gaining early exposure to both civil and criminal litigation, including high-stakes defence work, before refining her focus in real estate and banking at Seow & Megat under partners with decades of experience.

Her practice deepened in conveyancing and civil litigation at Y.C. Wong and Wong & Loh — advising on sub-sales, developer projects, tenancies, wills, banking and finance, divorce and probate, and contributing to Initial Public Offering (IPO) teams. She founded the firm with a clear vision: exceptional legal services built on trust, integrity and a personal approach.

Conveyancing & PropertyBanking & FinanceProbateFamily
Partner · Penang

Hee Ying Peng

LL.B (Hons) MMU / Advocate & Solicitor, Malaya

Called to the Malaysian Bar in August 2021, Mr. Hee began his career at Wong & Loh and later The Chambers of Yu Shin Ong, before being appointed Partner of the Penang branch on 25 April 2025. He has handled a broad range of civil litigation — debt recovery, contractual and landlord-tenant disputes, employment, defamation, fraud, probate, partnership and shareholder disputes, and construction litigation — with notable exposure to banking litigation.

Alongside his contentious work, he drafts and reviews commercial agreements, manages sub-sales, reassignments, wills and tenancies, and advises on employee handbooks following the 2023 Employment Act amendments. He also handles trademark registration with MyIPO, and serves as Legal Advisor to Persekutuan Persatuan Keturunan Xu Malaysia and the Ko Yang Tong Society Penang.

Civil LitigationCorporate & ConveyancingEmploymentIntellectual Property
04 — How We Can Help

Built for those we serve

For

Financial Institutions

  • Debt recovery and execution of judgments — bankruptcy, winding-up, injunctions, writs of execution, foreclosures and auctions.
  • Advisory on banking procedures, company and land laws, securities enforcement and stamping regulations.
  • Conventional and Islamic banking documentation for retail and corporate facilities.
For

Corporations

  • Civil and commercial litigation, from demand letters and trials to execution of judgments and settlement negotiations.
  • Corporate and commercial drafting — joint ventures, shareholders' and licensing agreements, M&A and asset sales.
  • Employment advice, due diligence, and schemes of arrangement.
For

Individuals

  • Civil litigation, debt collection, injunctions, specific performance and prohibitory orders.
  • Property transactions — sale and purchase, loans, tenancies and leases.
  • Matrimonial matters, criminal defence, wills, powers of attorney, probate and letters of administration.

Where considered counsel meets a warm welcome.
